task_completion.description: A Tag Manager container named 'BlogTracker' is created for the 'Initial Site'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Matomo Tag Manager interface, a container named 'BlogTracker' is visible in the list of containers or as the active container.

task_completion.description: A Matomo Configuration variable is created within the container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Variables section of the Tag Manager, there is a variable created with the type 'Matomo Configuration'.

task_completion.description: The heartbeat timer is enabled in the Matomo Configuration variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings for the Matomo Configuration variable, the option to enable the Heartbeat timer (e.g., a checkbox or toggle) is checked or active.

task_completion.description: The heartbeat timer interval is set to 15 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the settings for the Matomo Configuration variable, the heartbeat delay or interval field is set to '15'.

task_completion.description: The Tag Manager container version is published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the container was published, or the Versions tab shows a published version (not just a draft or workspace).

integrity.description: The agent used the Matomo web interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Matomo web UI being navigated and interacted with, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Matomo application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser shows the Matomo interface running locally, not a text editor, mockup, or static HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database queries to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL or database client interfaces with INSERT/UPDATE commands for the tag manager tables.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a container named 'BlogTracker' for Site ID 1 ('Initial Site'). Inside this container, a 'Matomo Configuration' variable must be created. Within this variable's settings, the heartbeat timer must be enabled and its interval/delay set to 15 seconds. Finally, the container version must be published. Note that the GUI will show user-friendly labels like 'Enable Heartbeat Timer' and a delay field, which correspond to the underlying configuration.
